fresh_facts.claim: Iran launched a new wave of missile attacks targeting Israel and Gulf Arab states on Thursday, April 2, 2026, with explosions heard in Dubai and sirens sounding in Bahrain.
President Trump announced that U.S. military operations against Iran are set to intensify over the next two to three weeks, stating American forces would strike Iran 'extremely hard.'
According to U.S. Central Command, more than 11,000 targets have been struck since Operation Epic Fury began on February 28, 2026.
Trump claimed that Iran's navy has been 'destroyed,' its air force is 'in ruins,' and its missile capabilities largely neutralized, with U.S. forces having crippled Iran's defense infrastructure.
A spokesman for Iran's military, Lt. Col. Ebrahim Zolfaghari, stated on Thursday that Tehran maintains hidden stockpiles of arms, munitions, and production facilities.

Nearly three dozen countries, including G7 members and Gulf partners, convened in a virtual meeting led by British officials to address the growing crisis and called on Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.
The Strait of Hormuz remains effectively sealed as of April 2-3, 2026.

More than 1,900 deaths have been reported in Iran, at least 19 in Israel, dozens across Gulf states and the West Bank, 13 U.S. service members killed, more than 1,200 in Lebanon, and over 1 million displaced.
Brent crude surged to approximately $108 per barrel, up nearly 50% since the conflict began, with disruptions in Strait of Hormuz shipping contributing to rising global energy prices.
Mojtaba Khamenei was appointed Supreme Leader after his father Ali Khamenei's death in U.S.-Israel strikes on February 28, 2026.
The IRGC has taken de facto control amid power struggles between President Masoud Pezeshkian and IRGC chief Ahmad Vahidi over war management and economic fallout.
Mohammad Bagher Zolghadr, a hardline ex-IRGC deputy, replaced killed Ali Larijani as Supreme National Security Council chief.
Severe medicine shortages persist in Iran, with insulin prices reaching approximately 70 million rials (~$46.70).
Dubai arrests of dozens of IRGC-linked money changers severely disrupted sanctions-evasion networks reliant on UAE.
Iran state TV warned citizens against revealing officials' hiding spots among civilians, portraying dissent as hostile and labeling protesters 'enemy combatants.'
A protest detainee named Amirhossein Hatami was executed on Thursday (March 31-April 2, 2026) for a Basij base fire during January protests.

President Trump announced productive talks with Iran and instructed a five-day postponement of attacks on Iranian energy sites, with envoys including JD Vance, Marco Rubio, Jared Kushner, and Steve Witkoff involved.
A 15-point U.S. peace plan was delivered via Pakistan proposing reopening the Strait of Hormuz, missile limits, sanctions relief, and nuclear rollback.
Trump claimed Iran released 20 cargo ships through the Strait as a negotiation sign, though experts attribute this to Iran's waterway control.
Iran's foreign ministry acknowledged messages from 'friendly states' while parliament speaker denied talks, indicating mixed internal messaging on negotiations.
New Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei threatened adversaries' security removal, with armed forces spokesperson warning attacks on global 'tourist centers.'
Approximately 2,000 additional U.S. Marines were expected in the region by Friday, April 3, 2026.

regime_fall.current_status: Iran faces escalating military pressure with 11,000+ strikes delivered as of April 2-3, while simultaneously experiencing internal fracturing under new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei, severe economic collapse including medicine shortages and disrupted sanctions-evasion networks, and contradictory diplomatic signals ranging from rejection of ceasefire to covert negotiation channels. The regime displays strategic ambiguity through mixed messaging on talks while maintaining rhetorical escalation and continuing military operations.
regime_fall.last_major_event: Trump announced productive talks with Iran and ordered a five-day postponement of attacks on energy sites while simultaneously rejecting a ceasefire and continuing Operation Epic Fury, as Iran launched new missile/drone attacks on April 2 and maintained the Strait of Hormuz closure despite international pressure from 30+ countries to reopen it.
